ddls:
CREATE TABLE "income_ranges" (
  "income_range_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each income range */,
  "income_range" TEXT /* Description of the income range (e.g., $20,000 to $24,999) */,
  "lower_bound" INTEGER /* Lower bound of the income range */,
  "upper_bound" INTEGER /* Upper bound of the income range */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("income_range_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location" TEXT /* Description of the location (e.g., New York City, NY) */,
  "city" TEXT /* City */,
  "state" TEXT /* State */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Geographic latitude of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Geographic longitude of the location */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the location */,
  "population_density" REAL /* Population density of the location */,
  "median_income" REAL /* Median income of the location */,
  "poverty_rate" REAL /* Poverty rate of the location */,
  "unemployment_rate" REAL /* Unemployment rate of the location */,
  "housing_median_value" REAL /* Median value of housing in the location */,
  "crime_rate" REAL /* Crime rate of the location */,
  "public_transportation_access" TEXT /* Accessibility to public transportation */,
  "healthcare_access" TEXT /* Accessibility to healthcare facilities */,
  "education_quality" TEXT /* Quality of education in the location */,
  "environmental_quality" TEXT /* Environmental quality of the location */,
  "climate_zone" TEXT /* Climate zone of the location */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the location */,
  "water_body_proximity" TEXT /* Proximity to water bodies */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "household_sizes" (
  "household_size_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each household size */,
  "household_size" INTEGER /* Number of people in the household */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("household_size_id")
)
column_names: Unique identifier for each income range
Description of the income range (e.g., $20,000 to $24,999)
Lower bound of the income range
Upper bound of the income range
Description of the location (e.g., New York City, NY)
Median value of housing in the location
Quality of education in the location
Unique identifier for each household size
Number of people in the household
